wins.title: Four in a Jeep
wins.plotSummary: In occupied postwar Vienna, an international four-power military patrol—American, British, French, and Soviet soldiers riding together in one jeep—is drawn into the case of an Austrian man who has escaped Soviet captivity and his desperate wife. As the patrol debates whether to help the fugitive's wife, tensions between the occupying powers play out in miniature within the jeep itself. Leopold Lindtberg's drama uses the four soldiers' uneasy cooperation to dramatize the fractured, multilateral control of postwar Vienna, ending when the Soviet soldier chooses conscience over orders.

wins.title: One Summer of Happiness
wins.plotSummary: University student Göran spends a summer at his uncle's farm and falls instantly in love with local girl Kerstin, but her strict, religious relatives and the town's disapproving vicar force the couple to hide their romance. Their brief, idyllic summer together is shadowed by Göran's looming return to university and the community's rigid moral judgment of their relationship. Arne Mattsson's film became internationally notorious for its nude scenes, which scandalized audiences even as the story itself mourns a love destroyed by small-town repression.

wins.title: The Wages of Fear
wins.plotSummary: Stranded in the poverty-stricken South American town of Las Piedras, four desperate European expatriates take a suicidal job driving two trucks loaded with unstable nitroglycerin over treacherous mountain roads to cap a raging oil well fire. Every pothole and misstep threatens instant death, and the grueling journey strips away each man's bravado, exposing the fear and desperation that drove them to accept the job in the first place. Henri-Georges Clouzot's thriller wrings unbearable tension from the drive itself, building to a finale as bleakly ironic as anything in noir cinema.

wins.title: Hobson's Choice
wins.plotSummary: Henry Hobson, a domineering, alcoholic Victorian bootshop owner in Salford, refuses to pay marriage settlements for his three grown daughters, expecting his capable eldest, Maggie, to keep working unpaid in his shop forever. Maggie instead proposes marriage to Will Mossop, the shop's talented but meek bootmaker, and the two set up a rival business that threatens to ruin her father. David Lean's comedy follows Maggie's calculated takeover of her own life, outmaneuvering her father and elevating Will from doormat to confident partner in the process.

wins.title: Die Ratten
wins.plotSummary: In early-1950s West Berlin, a destitute Polish refugee named Pauline, unable to care for her illegitimate baby, sells the child to Anna John, a childless woman desperate to be a mother. As the arrangement threatens to unravel amid jealousy, guilt, and the harsh realities of postwar poverty, both women are drawn into a spiraling conflict over the child neither can fully claim. Robert Siodmak's adaptation of Gerhart Hauptmann's play transplants a tenement tragedy into the rubble of postwar Germany.

wins.title: Invitation to the Dance
wins.plotSummary: Told entirely through dance and mime without a word of spoken dialogue, the film weaves together three unrelated stories, including a heartbroken circus clown pining for a fellow performer who loves an aerialist instead. Gene Kelly, directing solo for the first time, dances all three lead roles alongside some of the era's top ballet performers, including Igor Youskevitch and Tamara Toumanova. Though a box-office disappointment on its delayed 1956 release, the film stands as an ambitious experiment in wordless, dance-driven storytelling.

wins.title: 12 Angry Men
wins.plotSummary: On a sweltering summer day, twelve jurors retreat to deliberate the fate of a teenager accused of murdering his father, with a guilty verdict carrying a mandatory death sentence. Eleven jurors are ready to convict within minutes, but one holdout, unconvinced beyond reasonable doubt, forces the group to slow down and re-examine the evidence piece by piece. Sidney Lumet's single-room drama turns the jurors' escalating arguments into an anatomy of prejudice, groupthink, and conscience under pressure.

wins.title: Wild Strawberries
wins.plotSummary: Isak Borg, a cold, self-satisfied 78-year-old physician, drives from Stockholm to Lund to receive an honorary doctorate, accompanied by his estranged daughter-in-law Marianne, who is contemplating leaving his son. Along the way, vivid dreams, memories, and chance encounters with hitchhikers force Isak to confront the emotional distance and quiet cruelties that have defined his long life. Ingmar Bergman's road movie turns a single day's journey into a reckoning with mortality, regret, and the possibility of late-life tenderness.

wins.title: Les Cousins
wins.plotSummary: Naive provincial student Charles moves to Paris to live with his decadent, indifferent cousin Paul while both attend law school, and quickly falls for the alluring, promiscuous Florence from Paul's social circle. As Charles studies dutifully and Paul drifts through parties and idle seduction, Paul works, consciously or not, to undermine Charles's romance and pull Florence back into his own orbit. Claude Chabrol's drama pits innocence against decadence, building toward a devastating collision between the cousins' opposite ways of living.

wins.title: El Lazarillo de Tormes
wins.plotSummary: Lazarillo, a poor boy from a humble family, is entrusted by his desperate mother to a cruel blind beggar who teaches him to survive through cunning and deception. Passed from master to master, each more exploitative than the last, Lazarillo uses the wits and street-smarts he's forced to develop to endure poverty and abuse in 16th-century Spain. César Fernández Ardavín's adaptation of the anonymous picaresque novel follows Lazarillo's education in survival as a portrait of a society that offers the poor nothing but their own cleverness.

wins.title: La Notte
wins.plotSummary: Successful novelist Giovanni Pontano and his emotionally distant wife Lidia drift through a single day and night in Milan after visiting a dying friend in the hospital, moving from a chaotic book party to a wealthy industrialist's late-night gathering. Both quietly pursue and are pursued by others—Giovanni by a hospital patient and later a young woman at the party, Lidia by a former admirer—while their own marriage grows more hollow with each passing hour. Michelangelo Antonioni's drifting, plotless structure brings the couple's mutual alienation fully into view by dawn.

wins.title: A Kind of Loving
wins.plotSummary: Draughtsman Vic Brown begins a casual affair with co-worker Ingrid Rothwell in early-1960s Lancashire, and proposes marriage only after learning she's pregnant, despite his own ambivalence about her. The newlyweds move in with Ingrid's domineering mother, who disapproves of Vic at every turn, and the strain deepens after Ingrid suffers a miscarriage and Vic sinks into resentment and drink. John Schlesinger's kitchen-sink drama ends not with romance resolved but with the couple settling, without illusions, for what the title calls "a kind of loving."

wins.title: Bushido, Samurai Saga
wins.plotSummary: Spanning seven generations of a Japanese family from the founding of the Tokugawa shogunate to the early 1960s, the film traces how each generation's men sacrifice themselves and their loved ones in the name of absolute loyalty to lord, country, or company. The pattern repeats across centuries until Susumu, the last male heir, witnesses his own fiancée's suicide attempt and finally refuses to continue the family's cycle of blind devotion. Tadashi Imai's samurai epic uses the recurring tragedy to indict a code of loyalty that consumes everyone who serves it.

wins.title: To Bed or Not to Bed
wins.plotSummary: An Italian merchant travels to Sweden on business and finds himself bewildered and increasingly entangled by the sexual openness of Swedish society, which clashes comically with his own conservative expectations. Alberto Sordi's fish-out-of-water performance carries the film's culture-clash comedy as his character's business trip spirals into one embarrassing misadventure after another. Gian Luigi Polidoro's satire plays the Italian visitor's discomfort for broad comic effect throughout.

wins.title: Dry Summer
wins.plotSummary: Tobacco farmer Osman dams the spring that feeds his village's fields, choosing to hoard the water for his own crops despite his neighbors' furious objections and a legal order to keep it flowing. His gentler younger brother Hasan reluctantly goes along with the scheme even as it poisons relations with the rest of the village and his own new marriage. When sabotage of the dam turns violent, Osman lets Hasan take the blame for a killing that was his own doing, sealing the brothers' fates together.

wins.title: Alphaville
wins.plotSummary: Secret agent Lemmy Caution arrives in the technocratic city-state of Alphaville posing as a journalist, tasked with finding a missing agent, assassinating the city's creator, and destroying the sentient computer Alpha 60 that suppresses emotion and free thought among its citizens. As Caution navigates a populace stripped of poetry, love, and independent feeling, he falls for Natacha, the daughter of Alphaville's chief scientist, and works to awaken her buried humanity. Jean-Luc Godard shoots this science-fiction dystopia entirely in the ordinary streets and modernist buildings of contemporary Paris, using nothing but atmosphere to conjure the future.

wins.title: Cul-de-Sac
wins.plotSummary: Two wounded gangsters, fleeing a botched robbery, take refuge in the remote tidal-island castle of a neurotic Englishman, George, and his young, restless French wife, Teresa, holding the couple hostage while awaiting rescue. As one gangster dies of his wounds and the other settles in to wait, the power dynamics among the four twist into uneasy alliances, drunken camaraderie, and simmering sexual tension. Roman Polanski's black comedy escalates toward violence once the castle's isolation is broken by a group of George's oblivious houseguests.

wins.title: Le Départ
wins.plotSummary: Marc, a car-obsessed young hairdresser in Brussels, schemes to get his hands on a Porsche for an upcoming rally after his boss refuses to lend him one, trying everything from conning a dealer to seducing a wealthy client. Along the way he falls for Michèle, who joins his increasingly absurd quest, including a night locked inside the Brussels Motor Show and a comic disguise to share a hotel room. Jerzy Skolimowski's lighthearted comedy follows Marc's single-minded obsession as it collides, again and again, with his growing feelings for Michèle.

wins.title: Who Saw Him Die?
wins.plotSummary: A liberal-minded teacher in Sweden struggles to control and connect with a classroom of unruly, disengaged children, finding the demands of the job steadily wearing down his idealism. Per Oscarsson stars in this bleak, black-and-white portrait of a well-meaning educator ground down by an indifferent system and students he can't reach. Clas Engström adapted the screenplay from his own novel about the quiet collapse of one teacher's convictions.

wins.title: Early Works
wins.plotSummary: In the aftermath of Yugoslavia's 1968 student protests, three disillusioned young men and a woman named Jugoslava, inspired by Marxist texts, travel to remote villages and factories hoping to spark a genuine revolution among indifferent locals. Their idealism curdles into bitterness as their efforts fail and they're arrested, and upon release the men turn their rage on Jugoslava herself, murdering her as a witness to their failure. Želimir Žilnik's Yugoslav Black Wave film ends on a stark warning against half-measured revolution, framing the group's violence as the logical endpoint of ideology divorced from practice.

wins.title: The Garden of the Finzi-Continis
wins.plotSummary: In 1938 Ferrara, the wealthy Jewish Finzi-Contini family opens their walled estate to friends of their children Micòl and Alberto after Jews are banned from the city's tennis club. Giorgio, a Jewish businessman's son, has loved Micòl since adolescence, but she stays distant and eventually takes up with his gentile friend Giampiero instead. As Mussolini's racial laws tighten and war approaches, Giorgio drifts away from the family while Micòl clings to their insulated privilege. When Italy's Jews are rounded up in 1943, the Finzi-Continis are arrested together, and a last fleeting reunion between Giorgio and Micòl ends with their fates left uncertain.

wins.title: The Canterbury Tales
wins.plotSummary: Pier Paolo Pasolini adapts eight of Chaucer's tales, framed by pilgrims gathering at the Tabard Inn and traveling toward Canterbury, with Pasolini himself playing Chaucer. The vignettes range from the Merchant's Tale, in which an old blind husband is deceived by his young wife, to the Friar's Tale, expanded into a brutal account of a man burned alive for sodomy, to the slapstick Cook's Tale built around comic actor Ninetto Davoli. Loosely strung together rather than following Chaucer's pilgrim-narrator framing, the film mixes bawdy comedy with sudden cruelty to capture the earthiness of the original poem.

wins.title: Distant Thunder
wins.plotSummary: Set in a Bengal village during World War II, the film follows young Brahmin doctor-teacher Gangacharan and his wife Angana as the Great Famine of 1943 slowly closes in around them. Satyajit Ray, shooting in colour for one of the first times in his career, traces the famine's effects through the small rhythms of village life rather than spectacle, watching traditional social and caste norms erode as hunger spreads. What begins as a story of quiet domestic routine becomes a portrait of a catastrophe that killed millions, rendered entirely at human scale.

wins.title: The Apprenticeship of Duddy Kravitz
wins.plotSummary: Duddy Kravitz, a brash young Jewish man from a poor Montreal family, schemes his way toward wealth and his grandfather's respect, first hustling at a Laurentian resort hotel and then setting his sights on buying up land around a lake with his girlfriend Yvette. He raises capital by filming weddings and bar mitzvahs and by drawing a disabled acquaintance, Virgil, into a smuggling scheme, ultimately forging Virgil's signature to close his final land purchase. Duddy gets the property and success he wanted, but the ruthlessness of his climb costs him Yvette, Virgil's trust, and his grandfather's blessing.

wins.title: Adoption
wins.plotSummary: Kata, a 42-year-old factory worker in Hungary, lives alone near a children's home and longs for a child of her own, but the married man she's involved with refuses to father one with her. She grows close to Anna, a teenager from the orphanage, helping her marry her boyfriend over the institution's objections, and eventually decides to adopt a baby herself. Director Márta Mészáros, working in a near-documentary style, traces Kata's path from loneliness toward a family built outside convention.

wins.title: Buffalo Bill and the Indians, or Sitting Bull's History Lesson
wins.plotSummary: In 1885, Buffalo Bill Cody's Wild West show hires the real Chief Sitting Bull as a headline attraction, expecting him to play into the legend of the murderous savage that Cody has built his fame on. Instead Sitting Bull carries himself with quiet dignity and refuses to reenact Custer's Last Stand as anything but what it was, infuriating the vain Cody, who can no longer separate his invented persona from reality. Robert Altman's episodic, behind-the-scenes portrait of the show's performers and hangers-on turns the celebration of frontier heroism into a study of American self-mythology.

wins.title: The Ascent
wins.plotSummary: During World War II, two Soviet partisans, Sotnikov and Rybak, are captured by German forces and local collaborators while searching for food in occupied Belarus. Under torture, Sotnikov gives up nothing, while Rybak tells his captors what he thinks they already know, hoping to buy time to escape, and is offered a job as a police collaborator instead. Sotnikov chooses execution rather than betrayal, and Rybak, left alive but consumed by guilt, is unable even to end his own life, watched by the villagers who now despise him.

wins.title: Ascensor
wins.plotSummary: A short Spanish film in which four strangers become trapped together in a stalled elevator, their forced proximity exposing the frictions and tensions between them. Written and directed by Tomás Muñoz, it's one of the shortest and least-documented films ever to win the Golden Bear, and few details of its plot or production survive in general circulation today.

wins.title: Las truchas
wins.plotSummary: At a fishing club's annual dinner in late-Franco-era Spain, well-heeled members serve and eat visibly rotten trout rather than admit anything is wrong with the catch. José Luis García Sánchez uses the absurd dinner-party setup as a satire of middle-class denial and conformity in the dictatorship's final years. The dark comedy plays out almost entirely around a single table, letting the diners' willful blindness stand in for a broader social complacency.

wins.title: What Max Said
wins.plotSummary: The film centers on a man who feels cut off from the people around him, quietly withdrawing from family and social life in late-1970s Spain. Emilio Martínez Lázaro's understated character study won the Golden Bear, though it remains one of the festival's more obscure winners, with little of its plot or production history preserved beyond its central premise of alienation.

wins.title: David
wins.plotSummary: In 1930s Berlin, young David Singer comes of age as the son of a rabbi, watching his family and community come under mounting threat as Nazi persecution escalates. His brother Leo tries to hide his Jewish identity by wearing a Nazi uniform, while their father insists on pride in the face of humiliation, even after his synagogue is burned. As the campaign against Berlin's Jews intensifies, David works to raise money for the family's escape to Mandate Palestine, watching the city's Jewish community disappear around him.

wins.title: Heartland
studios: The National Endowment for the Humanities
wins.plotSummary: In 1910, widow Elinor and her young daughter travel to a remote Wyoming ranch, where Elinor takes work as housekeeper for reserved Scottish rancher Clyde Stewart. She resolves to file her own land claim, and marries Clyde so he'll help her build a house on it, settling into the harsh realities of homestead life. A brutal winter costs them half the herd and a newborn son to fever, but Elinor refuses to abandon the claim, choosing to stay and rebuild.

wins.title: Palermo or Wolfsburg
wins.plotSummary: Palermo or Wolfsburg follows Nicola, a young Sicilian man who leaves his rural hometown to seek work in the West German industrial city of Wolfsburg. Struggling against prejudice and alienation as an immigrant laborer, he becomes entangled with the German legal system, as reflected in the film's courtroom scenes. Director Werner Schroeter contrasts the warmth of Nicola's Sicilian origins with the coldness he encounters in industrial West Germany.

wins.title: Deprisa, deprisa
wins.plotSummary: Deprisa, deprisa follows a gang of young delinquents in Madrid, led by Pablo and Meca, who rob banks and businesses to fund a life of fast cars and reckless freedom. Pablo falls for a supermarket cashier, Ángela, who is drawn into the gang's escalating heists. As their crimes grow more violent, the group is picked off one by one, and the film ends with Ángela driving away alone with the stolen money after Pablo dies from his wounds.

wins.title: Veronika Voss
wins.plotSummary: Set in 1955 Munich, the film follows Veronika Voss, a once-famous UFA star now faded and forgotten, who begins an affair with sports journalist Robert Krohn. As Robert investigates her life, he discovers that Veronika is a morphine addict controlled by Dr. Marianne Katz, a corrupt neurologist who is slowly draining her of her wealth. Robert's attempts to expose the scheme fail, and Dr. Katz ultimately administers a fatal overdose after coercing Veronika into signing over her assets.

wins.title: Ascendancy
wins.plotSummary: Set in Ireland during and after the First World War, Ascendancy follows Connie Wintour, a young woman from the Protestant landowning class known as the Ascendancy. As the Irish War of Independence intensifies around her family's estate, Connie is gradually drawn into contact with the Irish nationalist movement, forcing her to confront the crumbling world of British rule in Ireland.

wins.title: La colmena
wins.plotSummary: Set in Madrid in the harsh years following the Spanish Civil War, La colmena weaves together the lives of dozens of characters -- café regulars, writers, prostitutes, and ordinary citizens -- who cross paths at the café La Delicia. Through this mosaic of interconnected stories, the film paints a portrait of a society struggling with poverty, repression, and quiet desperation under the early Franco regime.

wins.title: Love Streams
wins.plotSummary: Sarah Lawson, in the midst of a difficult divorce, moves in with her brother Robert Harmon, a hard-drinking writer who avoids emotional commitment despite his relationships with women. When Robert is forced to look after the young son he has never known, the visit unsettles both father and son, while Sarah tries to draw Robert out of his self-destructive isolation. As the siblings care for one another through their respective crises, the film builds to a surreal, dreamlike reckoning with love, loneliness, and family bonds.

wins.title: Wetherby
wins.plotSummary: In the Yorkshire town of Wetherby, schoolteacher Jean Travers hosts a dinner party attended by a mysterious young stranger, John Morgan. The next morning Morgan returns unannounced and fatally shoots himself at her kitchen table, sending Jean and her circle of friends searching for answers. Through interlocking flashbacks the film uncovers the histories of Jean, her guests, and Morgan himself, ultimately leading Jean to a reckoning with her own long-buried disappointments and losses.

wins.title: The Woman and the Stranger
wins.plotSummary: Based on Leonhard Frank's novella Karl und Anna, the film follows two friends, Karl and Richard, who grow close while held together in a German POW camp during the First World War. Richard escapes and makes his way to Karl's wife, Anna, eventually forming a relationship with her while posing as her missing husband. When the real Karl finally returns home, the three must confront the consequences of the deception.

wins.title: Stammheim
wins.plotSummary: Stammheim dramatizes the mid-1970s trial of the founding members of the Red Army Faction, also known as the Baader-Meinhof Group, held in a specially built courtroom adjoining Stammheim Prison in Stuttgart. Drawing heavily on actual trial transcripts, the film reconstructs the courtroom proceedings and the defendants' confrontational stance toward the West German state, as the legal system struggles to contain them.

wins.title: The Theme
wins.plotSummary: A successful but creatively hollow Soviet playwright, Kim Yesenin, travels to the historic town of Suzdal in search of inspiration, privately tormented by how conformist and mediocre his work has become. There he meets Sasha, a museum guide who bluntly tells him what she thinks of his plays, and becomes a secret witness to her tearful goodbye to a dissident lover emigrating to the United States. Shaken, Yesenin crashes his car while driving back to Moscow at night, and the film ends on his unresolved fate as he loses consciousness mid-phone call to Sasha.

wins.title: Red Sorghum
wins.plotSummary: Set in rural Shandong province from the 1920s through the Second Sino-Japanese War, Red Sorghum follows Jiu'er, a young woman married off to an elderly, leprous distillery owner. After bandits attack her wedding procession, one of her sedan-chair carriers rescues her and later becomes her lover, and together they take over the distillery following her husband's death. Years later, as Japanese forces invade the region, Jiu'er and the distillery workers mount a desperate ambush against the occupiers, with tragic consequences.

wins.title: Rain Man
wins.plotSummary: Charlie Babbitt, a fast-talking Los Angeles car dealer, learns that his estranged father has died and left most of his multi-million-dollar estate to an institutionalized brother Charlie never knew existed: Raymond, an autistic savant. Charlie takes Raymond on a cross-country road trip in an attempt to gain control of the inheritance, but along the way discovers Raymond's extraordinary abilities and the childhood bond they once shared. By the journey's end, Charlie's motives shift from greed toward genuine love for his brother.

wins.title: Larks on a String
wins.plotSummary: Larks on a String (Czech: Skřivánci na niti) is a 1969 Czech film directed by Jiří Menzel. The film was banned by the Czechoslovak government, but was later released in 1990 after the fall of the Communist regime. It tells the stories of various characters considered bourgeois by Czechoslovakia's communist government in the 1950s, who have been forced to work in a junkyard for the purposes of re-education.

wins.title: Music Box
wins.plotSummary: Hungarian immigrant Michael J. Laszlo faces a trial and having his U.S. citizenship revoked after being accused of war crimes during the Second World War. Laszlo insists it is mistaken identity. His daughter, Ann Talbot, a defense attorney, resolves to defend her father in court.
